name: Sweet Pepper & Basil Frittata
recipeIngredient: 4 medium Sweet bell peppers, preferably a combination of red and yellow, thinly sliced (about 4 cups)
1/2 cup Fresh basil leaves, bundled and thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
From "Fields of Greens" by Annie Somerville (Bantam Books, 1993, $26.95)
First, reduce balsamic vinegar by half in a saucepan over high heat. Set aside.
Heat one tablespoon of olive oil in a large skillet; add onion, half a teaspoon of salt and a few pinches of pepper. Sauté onion over medium heat until it begins to release its juices, about 4-5 minutes.
Add peppers, garlic, and bay leaf; cover and cook very slowly over low heat for about 15 minutes. Set vegetables aside. Remove the bay leaf.
Preheat oven to 325°F.
Beat eggs in a bowl and add onion-pepper mixture, cheeses, and basil. Season with remaining quarter teaspoon of salt and a few more pinches of pepper.
In a 9-inch sauté pan with an ovenproof handle, heat the remaining tablespoon of olive oil to just below smoking point. Swirl oil around sides of the pan to coat it. Turn the heat down to low, then immediately pour frittata mixture into the pan.
Cook the frittata over low heat for 1-2 minutes until the sides begin to set; transfer to a preheated oven at 325°F and bake, uncovered, for 20-25 minutes, until the frittata is golden and firm. Loosen the frittata with a spatula.
Place a plate over the pan, flip it, and turn the frittata out. Brush the bottom and sides with reduced balsamic vinegar and cut into wedges.
